Looking at the images, the player (green square) has moved up - it's now closer to the target at (36,32). The target is still visible as a blue square. Let me check if the target has changed state (highlighted/disappeared) by examining the segmentation.
From the images, I can see:
- Step 402-403: Player at bottom, target visible
- Step 404: Player moved up slightly
- Step 405: Player moved up more, now very close to target
The target at (36,32) is still visible as a blue square. Let me check if pressing SPACE now will collect it. But first, let me verify the player's current position and whether the target is in a "highlighted" state.
Actually, looking at the images more carefully, the player seems to have moved up and is now right below the target. The target is still visible. Let me try pressing SPACE to collect it.
But wait - I need to be more careful. Let me check the segmentation to see the current state of objects.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state - player position and target states
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] >= 3:  # Skip single-pixel noise
        # Get bounding box center
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min_r},{min_c})-({max_r},{max_c}), hash={n['hash']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
